steps.n: These are individual letters that appear at the beginning of some surahs in the Quran. While their exact meaning is known only to Allah, we can identify them as the names of the Arabic letters Alif (ا), Lam (ل), and Ra (ر).

steps.m: Individual letters Alif-Lam-Ra (opening of سورة الحجر)
steps.n: The letters are written connected with a special mark (مد) over the Alif to show this is a special Quranic opening, not a regular word.
This is the base part of the demonstrative pronoun that means 'that' or 'those'

steps.n: The كَ at the end shows we're pointing to something far away. In this ayah, it's pointing to the verses (ءَايٰتُ) that will be mentioned next.

steps.n: This is a particle that introduces a hypothetical situation that didn't happen. In this ayah, it shows the disbelievers wishing they were Muslims, but this wish comes too late.

Adding وا at the end makes it talk about a group instead of one person. This word tells us who is doing the action in يَوَدُّ (wishes/loves).

Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and make it a noun

Added كَ to show this Lord belongs to you (speaking to one male). This word comes after إِنَّ (indeed/verily) which makes it an object in the sentence.
This is a basic pronoun that points to a male person or thing we're talking about. In this ayah, it refers back to رَبَّكَ (your Lord) and is the subject of the verb يَحْشُرُ (he gathers).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to show the action is happening now or will happen

steps.m: he gathers/will gather them (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who is being gathered. In this ayah, "them" refers back to the disbelievers mentioned in previous verses.
This is a special word that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain, like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English
Added هُۥ (meaning 'he') to show who is being talked about. This 'he' is described by the words حَكِيمٌ عَلِيمٌ (wise, knowing) that come after.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Forms an adjective meaning 'characterized by wisdom/good judgment'
The ٌ (tanween) mark was added to make it a complete noun describing Allah's perfect wisdom

steps.m: one who knows completely (attribute of حَكِيمٌ)
steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters and vowels to show this describes someone who has complete knowledge. This word follows حَكِيمٌ (All-Wise) as another attribute.
The tanween (ٌ) shows this is a general description of complete knowledge, often used as one of Allah's names
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation
Added ل at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'truly' or 'surely'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it, like saying 'and'

steps.m: to create from nothing, to make something new

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Adding نا changes it from 'he created' to 'we created'

steps.m: to be close and familiar with others

steps.n: Changes the basic meaning into a noun for the creature that is naturally social and familiar with others
Adds ال to show we're talking about humans as a specific category (object of خَلَقْنَا - we created)
Shows this word is receiving the action of خَلَقْنَا (we created)
Basic preposition showing that the dry clay (صَلْصٰلٍ) comes from or is made of dark mud (حَمَإٍ)

steps.m: to make a clanking or ringing sound (like dry clay)
clay that makes a ringing sound
steps.n: The root letters combine with fatha and sukoon to make a noun meaning 'clay that rings' - like when you tap dry clay and it makes a sound

steps.m: clay that makes a ringing sound (in genitive case)
steps.n: Adding the two dots under the last letter shows this word is receiving the action in the sentence

steps.m: dry clay that makes a sound when tapped (owned, after مِن)
steps.n: The alif (ا) becomes a long alif (ٰ), and the word gets kasra tanween (ٍ) because it comes after مِن (from/of)
This is a basic particle that shows something comes from somewhere else or belongs to something

The م gets doubled (takes a shadda) because it comes after a similar sound. This is just a pronunciation change and doesn't affect the meaning. This word connects to صَلْصٰلٍ (clay) to tell what humans were made from.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Added tanween kasra at the end to show this describes what kind of clay (صَلْصٰلٍ) humans were made from

steps.m: to shape or mold something smoothly

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changes from a verb (shaped) to describe something that was shaped/molded by adding ma- at the start and -oon at the end
The doubled م adds emphasis to show the molding/shaping was done thoroughly
The tanween at the end shows this word is describing the black mud mentioned in the ayah
The doubled ن at the end shows intensity of hiding

steps.m: the one who is hidden (the jinn)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific

steps.m: and the jinn (object of خَلَقْنٰهُ/we created)
steps.n: Added و at start to mean 'and'. This word is what was created (object) in the phrase 'we created it'

steps.m: to create or form something from nothing

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha vowels to show this happened in the past
Added sukun to prepare for attaching pronouns
Adding نا shows that 'we' did the action
Added هُ meaning 'him' which refers back to الجان (the jinn) mentioned just before this word

steps.m: from (showing what the jinn were created from)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows the source or origin of something. Here it introduces the material (نَّارِ ٱلسَّمُومِ - scorching fire) that the jinn were created from.

steps.m: to be in front of, to precede

steps.n: Adding vowel marks to create an adverb meaning 'before'. In this ayah, it tells us when the jinn was created - before the scorching fire.

steps.m: from (shows where something comes from, in this case the جَآنَّ was created from نَّارِ ٱلسَّمُومِ)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it's used to show what the jinn was created from.
The و (weak letter) changes to ا to make it easier to say. This word means fire because fire gives light and heat.
The shadda (ّ) on ن makes the word stronger, showing this is an intense or powerful fire. This fire is specifically described as السَّمُومِ (the scorching fire).
The ِ at the end shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else (السَّمُومِ - the scorching wind)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed verb into noun showing something with intense quality
Adding ال makes it refer to the specific scorching wind mentioned in the context of creating jinn (from نَّارِ ٱلسَّمُومِ - fire of the scorching wind)

steps.m: of the intensely hot/poisonous wind (describing نَّارِ/fire)
steps.n: The kasra shows this word describes what kind of fire (نَّارِ) the jinn were created from
This is a time word that shows when something happened in the past. Here it introduces what your Lord said to the angels.
The و connects this story to previous stories in the Quran, showing it's part of a sequence of events being told
Added fathas to make it past tense. This shows someone (he) did the action in the past.

steps.m: he said (subject of لِلْمَلٰٓئِكَةِ - to the angels)
steps.n: The middle letter و changed to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to say.
The doubled ب (with shadda) shows this is someone who takes complete care of others
The ُ shows this word is doing something in the sentence - here it's doing the action of قَالَ (said)

steps.m: your Lord (who said to the angels)
steps.n: Added كَ which means 'your', showing this Lord belongs to or is connected to the one being spoken to. This Lord is the one who said (قَالَ) something to the angels.
Adding vowel marks creates the basic past tense verb
This is not correct - 'malaa'ik' (angels) comes from 'malak' (angel), not from 'malaka' (to own/possess). They are different words.
Added feminine ending to complete the plural form
Added 'the' to make it definite

steps.m: to/for the angels (who Allah speaks to)
steps.n: Added لِ to show 'to/for' and kasra at end to show connection to what follows
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger, like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English
Added ى at the end to show 'I' am speaking. The نّ gets a kasra (ِ ) sound before ى. This is Allah speaking to the angels in this ayah.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters together
Forms the doer of the action. This word describes what Allah is saying about Himself to the angels after إِنِّي (indeed I am).
The double damma (ٌ) makes it mean 'a creator' instead of 'the creator'. The long 'aa' sound is written with a special mark (ٰ) in the Quran.
Connected the letters with fatha marks to create the noun form referring to humans (who have visible skin)
Added tanwin alif to show this is the thing being created. In this ayah, Allah is telling the angels 'I am creating (خٰلِقٌۢ) a human being (بَشَرًا)'.
This is a preposition showing where something comes from. In this ayah, it shows that the clay (صَلْصٰلٍ) comes from black mud (حَمَإٍ)
The shadda (ّ) is added because the previous word صَلْصٰلٍ ends with ٍ (tanween kasra). When مِن comes after a word ending with ن or tanween, the م gets a shadda to help with smooth pronunciation.

steps.m: to make a ringing or clanging sound

steps.n: Root letters were connected with vowels to make a noun meaning clay that makes a ringing sound when hit
Added the genitive case ending to show this word is the object of a preposition

steps.m: of a clay that rings (when struck)
steps.n: The alif changed to a long alif (ٰ), while keeping the tanwin kasra that shows this describes what something is made of

steps.m: from (showing what something is made of)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing what something is made from. In this ayah, it introduces the material that the human was created from.
The letter م gets a shadda (double mark) to emphasize where something came from, like saying 'made FROM clay' with extra emphasis
Added short vowels (fat-ha) to connect the root letters to make the word for 'black mud'
Added tanwin kasra to show this describes what kind of clay/صَلْصٰلٍ is being talked about. The إ is just a different way to write the hamza.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (shaped) to describe something that was shaped/molded, like clay
Added emphasis to first letter with shadda

steps.m: something that was shaped/made smooth (describing حَمَإٍ - black mud)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra to show this describes the black mud mentioned in the ayah
This is a basic conditional time word meaning 'when' - it is not derived from a root
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it, showing sequence or result

steps.m: to make something balanced, even, or complete

steps.n: Added shadda to show extra strength in the action, and added vowel marks to make it a past-tense verb
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action. The ى changes to يْ before adding تُ

steps.m: I fashioned him/it completely (refers to آدَمَ/Adam mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هُ with prolongation mark ۥ to show who received the action. This refers to Adam who Allah fashioned completely.

steps.m: to blow into something or breathe life into it

steps.n: Added fatha vowels to show this happened in the past. The word نَفَخ shows the basic action of blowing or breathing into something.
Adding تُ at the end shows that 'I' did this action. This refers to Allah breathing life into His creation.

steps.m: and I blew/breathed (into him, after سَوَّيْتُهُ - I fashioned him)
steps.n: Adds وَ (and) at the start to connect this action with the previous action of fashioning mentioned in سَوَّيْتُهُ. This shows the sequence: first Allah fashioned the creation, then breathed into it.
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment
Added pronoun هِ to show who/what is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to Adam's form that Allah created.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from. In this ayah, it connects to رُّوحِى (my spirit) to show the spirit comes from Allah.
The vowel pattern makes it a noun meaning 'spirit'
Adding ي at the end makes it mean 'my'
Added ى at the end to show the spirit belongs to the speaker (Allah in this context)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The و at the start is dropped, and وا is added at the end to make it a command for a group
Added فَ at the beginning to show this action follows from what was mentioned before (when I have breathed into him)
Added fatha to show the basic preposition meaning

steps.m: to/for him (connected to فَقَعُوا۟ - fall down)
steps.n: Added pronoun هُ (him) with extension mark ۥ. This preposition phrase shows who should be fallen down to in prostration.

steps.m: to bow down with your forehead touching the ground

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past-tense verb
Changed from a past-tense verb (he prostrated) to a noun describing someone who does the prostrating. Like changing 'ran' to 'runner'.
Added ين to show this refers to multiple people prostrating

1. Changed alif to alif with line above (سٰ) which is a writing variation
2. Added the final fatha mark to show this word is being acted upon by the command 'fall' (فَقَعُوا)

steps.m: he bowed down in worship (subject of أَجْمَعُونَ)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word is connected to أَجْمَعُونَ (all together) in this ayah.

steps.m: so/then he bowed down in worship
steps.n: Adding فَ shows this prostration happened as a result of or immediately after something mentioned before
Added vowel marks (fatha) to the root letters to form the word for 'angel' - a special messenger of God
The word for 'angel' changes to make it plural by adding letters in the middle (alif and hamza) and ending with kasra
Adding taa marboota completes the plural form
Makes the noun definite. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb فَسَجَدَ (then they prostrated) in this ayah.
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Adding ُ shows this word is the subject doing the action in سَجَدَ (prostrated)
Adding هُمْ shows this refers specifically to the angels (ٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةُ) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed to pattern أَفْعَل to mean 'most/all' instead of just 'gather'

steps.m: all together (describing the angels in فَسَجَدَ ٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةُ)
steps.n: The ون ending shows this describes a group of angels mentioned earlier in the verse
This is an exception particle (أداة استثناء). The shadda indicates merging of ن+ل. The final alif is lengthened for emphasis.
The shadda shows that two similar letters (ن+ل) merged together, making the 'l' sound stronger
The alif is lengthened to show emphasis and pause before mentioning Iblis, who is being excluded from the group of those who prostrated
This is the basic form of the name Iblis, which refers to Satan. It comes from a word meaning 'to lose hope' because Satan lost hope in Allah's mercy.

steps.m: Satan (as the subject of أَبَىٰٓ - he refused)
steps.n: The fatha (َ) at the end shows that Iblis is the one doing the action in this ayah - he is the one who refused.
The root letters show the basic concept of refusing
Added vowel marks to show this is a past tense verb about 'he'
The final ي changes to ى with an alif above it (ٰ) because it's at the end of the word
Added a small alif (ٓ) above to help with pronunciation when continuing to the next word
This is a connecting word that links 'he refused' (أَبَىٰ) with what he refused to do - 'to be' (يَكُونَ). It works like 'to' in English when we say 'he refused to be'.
The root letters show the basic concept of being or existing
Basic vowels (fatha) added to make past tense verb meaning 'he was'
Added يَ at start to show present tense, and changed vowels to show ongoing action
Changed the ending vowel to fatha to show subjunctive mood
This is a basic preposition showing being together with something. In this ayah, it connects Iblis with those who prostrated.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed to describe someone who does the action
Made the word plural to show multiple people who prostrate
Added 'the' (ٱل) at the start and doubled the س. The ending َ shows it goes with the word 'with' (مَعَ)
Added vowels to make 'he said' - this is what Allah said to Iblis. The fatha marks show it's past tense.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs

steps.m: Iblis (the name of the devil who refused to bow to Adam)
O Iblis! (being addressed by Allah in قَالَ - He said)
steps.n: Added يٰٓ at the beginning to show someone is being called or addressed directly, like saying 'Hey!' in English. Added ُ at the end to show this is the main subject being addressed. This word comes after قَالَ (He said) where Allah is directly addressing Iblis.
This is an interrogative particle used here to ask about Iblees's condition or reason. In this ayah, it's followed by لَكَ (with you) to ask 'what is wrong with you?'
Starting with the basic preposition ل which shows direction or purpose
Added كَ to show who is being addressed. In this ayah, Allah is speaking to Iblis.
This is a particle that introduces a new clause and shows purpose or result
Added the negative particle لا to negate what comes after

steps.m: that not (introduces why إِبْلِيسُ is being questioned)
steps.n: The نْ combines with ل creating a shadda (doubled letter). This is a common pronunciation rule when these letters come together.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form
Added تَ at beginning to make it present tense 'is/are', and changed middle vowels to show it's happening now
Final vowel changes from 'u' to 'a' because أَلَّا (that not) comes before it, making it mean 'that you be' instead of just 'you are'
This is a particle showing being together with something/someone. Here it shows Iblis was asked why he wasn't with those who prostrated.

steps.m: to prostrate, to bow down in worship

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense verb meaning 'he prostrated'. Uses basic فَعَلَ verb pattern with fathas.
Changes from verb (he prostrated) to noun meaning 'one who prostrates/a prostrator'
Added emphasis to the س with shadda because it comes after ال
Added ين to make it plural, showing it refers to multiple people prostrating
Added ال to make it definite and َ at the end because it comes after the word مَعَ (with)
Changed from basic root 'say' to 'he said' by adding vowel marks. This word tells us who will do the prostrating.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs with و in the middle
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of the verb after it (أَكُن) to show it didn't happen in the past. It's like adding 'did not' in English.
Connected root letters with fatha to make past tense verb
Changed from past tense verb 'was' to noun meaning 'being/existence'
Added أَ at beginning to show first person (I)
The word 'I am' gets shorter after لَمْ (not) by dropping its last vowel and middle و

steps.m: to prostrate, bow down in worship

steps.n: Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he prostrated' to present tense command 'prostrate!' by changing the vowels
Added أَ at beginning to show first person singular (I)

steps.m: that I prostrate (to لِبَشَرٍ/to a human)
steps.n: Added لِّ (lam with shaddah) to show purpose/intent. This connects to لِبَشَرٍ (to a human) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the noun meaning 'human being'

steps.m: to/for a human being (object of لِأَسْجُدَ)
steps.n: Added لِ to show this is what Iblis refused to prostrate to, connected to the verb لِأَسْجُدَ (to prostrate to)

steps.m: to/for a human being (indefinite, object of لِأَسْجُدَ)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this refers to any human being, not a specific one

steps.m: to create or make something from nothing

steps.n: Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to show this happened in the past
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did this action instead of 'he'. Also added sukun (ْ) before the تَ to help pronunciation.
Added هُۥ at the end to show who was created (him). This word connects to لِّأَسْجُدَ (to prostrate) in the ayah, showing who Iblis refused to prostrate to.
Basic preposition showing what something is made from or comes from. In this ayah, it shows that the صَلْصٰلٍ (clay) is made from حَمَإٍ (dark mud).

steps.m: to make a ringing or clanking sound (like dry clay)
dry clay that makes a ringing sound
steps.n: The ا after ل shows this is something that makes this sound repeatedly

steps.m: dry clay that makes a ringing sound when struck
steps.n: Changed the alif to an alif maqsurah for spelling variation

steps.m: of dry clay that makes a ringing sound when struck (possessed by مِن)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra because this word comes after مِن (from) which requires the possessed thing to take this ending
Basic particle that shows something comes from or is made of something else. Here it shows what the human was created from.
Added shadda because it comes after another م sound. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowel marks to the root letters to make the word for black mud

steps.m: of black mud (describing what صَلْصٰلٍ was made from)
steps.n: Added the genitive case ending because it follows مِن (from) in the phrase مِن صَلْصٰلٍ مِّنْ حَمَإٍ (from clay from black mud)

steps.m: to shape or mold into a smooth form

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (doing the shaping) to describe something that received the shaping action

steps.m: of altered black mud that has been perfectly shaped/molded (describing حَمَإٍ)
steps.n: Added shadda for emphasis and tanween to show this word describes حَمَإٍ (black mud) in the ayah
Added vowel marks to show this is past tense. This word is connected to the next word فَٱخْرُجْ (so get out) as it introduces what was said.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to pronounce.
Changed from basic root meaning 'exit' to a command by adding marks - the ُ (damma) in middle tells you it's a command to one person
Added helper alif with damma at start to make the command easier to pronounce
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this command with what came before it in the verse (قَالَ - he said). The alif changes to a different form (ٱ) because it comes after فَ.
This is a preposition that shows something coming from or originating at somewhere. In this ayah, it connects to Allah commanding Iblis to leave/exit.
Added ها to refer back to Paradise/Heaven (which is feminine in Arabic). This connects to Allah commanding Iblis to exit from Paradise.
This is a particle that adds emphasis to a statement
Added كَ to show this is addressing the one being spoken to (Iblis) in this ayah

steps.m: for indeed you (addressing Iblis after being told to leave)
steps.n: Added فَ to connect this with what came before

steps.m: to throw stones at, to drive away
he threw stones at, he drove away
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed the verb into an adjective describing someone who receives this action repeatedly

steps.m: an outcast, one who is driven away (description of إِنَّكَ - indeed you are)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this is describing Shaytan in a general way, connecting to 'indeed you are' (إِنَّكَ) in the ayah

steps.m: indeed/certainly (emphasizes عَلَيْكَ ٱللَّعْنَةَ - the curse is upon you)
steps.n: This is a word that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain. It's like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English.

steps.m: and indeed (emphasizes عَلَيْكَ ٱللَّعْنَةَ - the curse is upon you)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of, affecting, or directed at something else. It's like saying 'on' or 'upon' in English.

steps.m: upon you (object of curse in this ayah)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this affects 'you' (when talking to one male person). In this ayah, it shows who the curse is directed at.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make it a past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun that means one specific instance of cursing
Added 'the' (ال) at the start, which made the ل double in sound
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by عَلَيْكَ (upon you)
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or endpoint. It connects one thing to another, like showing where something is going or until when it lasts.
Added a small alif (ىٰ) at the end to show how to pronounce the word correctly when continuing to read. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make the basic noun meaning 'day'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to the next word الدِّينِ (the judgment), making the phrase 'Day of Judgment'

steps.m: to judge or follow a way
steps.n: This becomes a noun meaning 'religion/judgment'
The kasra and yaa together make this into a noun meaning a system or way of life
The shadda emphasizes the د, making the meaning stronger
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific religion (Islam in this context)
The kasra at the end shows this connects to the word يَوْمِ (day), making the phrase 'Day of Judgment'
Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding the vowel marks 'fatha' (َ). This shows someone did the action of speaking in the past.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to say.

steps.m: to care for, raise, nurture completely

steps.n: Doubling the ب makes it stronger - shows complete lordship/mastery
The kasra at the end shows this is being directly addressed in speech after قَالَ (he said). The speaker is claiming a personal relationship with their Lord.
Changed to command form by adding initial alif with damma, sukun on middle letter, and changing final vowel
Changed from 'look!' to 'give respite!' by changing vowels
Added فَ to connect this request to the previous statement (رَبِّ - 'My Lord')

steps.m: so give me time! (as a command to Allah)
steps.n: Added نِى meaning 'me' to show who is asking for time. The long vowel at the end (ىٓ) shows emphasis in the request.
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or movement towards something
The alif at the end is written with a small mark above it (called an alif maqsurah). This is just a writing variation and doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters together with fatha and sukun to form the basic noun meaning 'day'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is in the genitive case, like after 'to/of/in'

steps.m: to send forth or raise up

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from active (he sent) to passive (being sent) by adding يُ at start and changing vowels - shows something is happening to someone
Added ون to show multiple people are being raised up

steps.m: he said (followed by فَإِنَّكَ - so indeed you)
steps.n: Basic root ق-و-ل (to speak) changed to past tense قَوَلَ (he said) by adding the vowel marks َ (fatha). Links to فَإِنَّكَ which shows what was said.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in middle)
This is a special word that adds emphasis, like saying 'truly' or 'certainly'. The shadda (ّ) makes the ن sound stronger to show this emphasis.

steps.m: so indeed (connects to قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: The فَ shows this is a response or result of what came before (قَالَ - he said). It's like saying 'then' or 'so' in English.

Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what comes before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense 'we' form. This verb connects to أَنَّكَ (that you) in this ayah, showing what we know about.
This is an emphatic particle that introduces and emphasizes what comes after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes 'we know that...'

steps.m: indeed you (object of نَعْلَمُ - we know)
steps.n: Added the pronoun كَ meaning 'you' (masculine singular). This whole phrase أَنَّكَ is the object of نَعْلَمُ (we know), meaning 'we know indeed you...'
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters
Changes from an adjective (tight) to a verb (becomes tight) by adding يَ at the start
The word gets a 'u' ending because it's connected to 'they say' (يَقُولُونَ) - showing how the Prophet feels when they speak
Added fatha and sukun to make the basic noun form. This word is what becomes tight/constricted in the phrase يَضِيقُ صَدْرُكَ (your chest becomes tight).

steps.m: your chest (subject of يَضِيقُ - becomes tight)
steps.n: Added ُ (damma) before كَ to show this is doing the action, then added كَ at the end to show it belongs to 'you' (masculine singular). This creates the complete subject of يَضِيقُ (becomes tight/constricted).
Starting with the basic interrogative particle that means 'what'
Added the preposition بِ (with/by) to show how something is done. In this ayah, it connects to يَقُولُونَ (they say) to show Allah knows what they are saying.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels. The middle weak letter و changes to ا in past tense.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle weak letter returns to و in present tense.
Removing the final ن sound but keeping the same meaning of 'they say'
Adding the fatha (َ) at the end completes the present tense verb form
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes the command more emphatic
Added فَ to show this command follows from previous context
Changed from basic root letters to the noun 'praise' by adding the vowel marks (fat-ha and sukoon)

steps.m: with praise (connected to فَسَبِّحْ - so glorify)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show how the glorifying should be done - with praise
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word that comes after it (your Lord)

steps.m: one who cares for and helps grow
steps.n: Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter to show this is about the one who does the caring
Added kasra under the doubled letter to show this lord belongs to someone

steps.m: your Lord (possessed by حَمْدِ in بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ)
steps.n: Added ك at the end to show this is your Lord. This word is connected to حَمْدِ (praise) in the phrase 'by the praise of your Lord'
Changed root 'to be' into a command by adding damma (u sound) and making it short - like saying 'Be!'

steps.m: and be! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at beginning to connect this command with the previous command فَسَبِّحْ (so glorify)
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or what group it belongs to
The م gets doubled (shown by ّ) because it comes before ال in السَّاجِدِينَ
The َ at the end connects this word to the next word السَّاجِدِينَ (those who prostrate)

steps.m: to prostrate, to bow down in worship

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed the word to describe someone who does this action
Added ين to make it plural (more than two)
Added ٱل to make it definite ('the prostrators'). The word comes after مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah, which is why it has the special ending.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he worshipped' to 'worship!' by adding ٱ at the start and changing the middle vowel to 'u' - this makes it a command
Added و at the start to mean 'and', connecting this command to previous instructions. This is Allah commanding His Messenger (رَسُول) to worship.

steps.m: Lord (one who cares for and nurtures)
steps.n: The doubled ب (shown by ّ) makes it mean someone who does a lot of caring and nurturing
Added كَ at the end to show it belongs to 'you'. This word is what is being worshipped in the command 'worship' (وَٱعْبُدْ).
Connected the base letters with fatha marks to form the basic particle
Added shadda to emphasize the middle letter

steps.m: until (connects to يَأْتِيَكَ - comes to you)
steps.n: Added alif maqsurah (ىٰ) at the end to extend the final sound and show this word connects to what comes after it
Connected the root letters together
Added يَ at start to show present tense, with أْ (sukun) and ِ (kasra) to complete the present tense form. This verb comes after حَتَّىٰ (until) in the ayah.
Added كَ at the end to show the action is happening to 'you'. The fatha (َ) before كَ helps connect the sounds.

steps.m: to be completely sure about something

steps.n: Added short vowels (fatha and kasra) to turn the root letters into 'he became certain' in the past tense
Added ي to make it into a noun meaning the state or quality of being certain

steps.m: the absolute certainty (subject of يَأْتِيَكَ - comes to you)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ُ to show it's doing the action of 'coming' in يَأْتِيَكَ
